words.example: Smartphones have become ubiquitous in modern life.

words.meaning: To make something less severe, serious, or painful.
words.example: The new policy aims to mitigate the impact of the drought.

words.meaning: Sternness or severity of manner; difficult economic conditions created by government measures to reduce spending.
words.example: The nation adopted austerity to cut its fiscal deficit.

words.meaning: Not securely held or in position; dangerously likely to fall or fail.
words.example: The climbers were in a precarious position on the cliff.

words.meaning: To reach a climax or point of highest development.
words.example: Years of training culminated in an Olympic gold.

words.meaning: Able to recover quickly from difficulties; tough.
words.example: A resilient economy bounces back after a shock.

words.meaning: Open to more than one interpretation; not having one obvious meaning.
words.example: The treaty's ambiguous wording led to disputes.
The accounts came under close scrutiny by auditors.
The floods were of unprecedented severity.
Relations between the two nations deteriorated rapidly.
